prompt: Create a cinematic surreal photorealistic image of a teenage Japanese schoolgirl in a classic sailor uniform floating or falling upward into the sky, viewed from a slightly high, diagonal angle. She is centered in the frame with arms stretched wide like she is surrendering to flight, long dark hair blown dramatically across the scene, wearing a pale blue sailor blouse with a navy collar, a deep burgundy scarf tie, a dark pleated skirt, knee-high black socks, and black shoes. Her face is intentionally hidden by a soft gray square censor block, while her head tilts back toward the sky. The world is inverted: an upside-down modern city skyline and reflective water-like surface occupy the top of the image, as if the sky and city have been mirrored above her. The background is a cold blue-gray cloudy sky with a dreamlike, melancholic atmosphere. Include exactly 6 black bird silhouettes flying around her: 1 large bird on the left, 2 smaller birds in the lower-left area, 1 small bird near the upper-right center, 1 small bird on the far right, and 1 large bird in the lower-right area. Use muted blue-gray and desaturated cyan tones, high-detail cinematic lighting, soft film grain, slight motion blur in the hair and birds, realistic fabric folds, and a mysterious emotional mood suggesting the phrase to the sky. No text, no watermark, square 1:1 composition.
